data.title: Alkimi Develops Transparent Rewards Engine on Sui Network
data.description: Alkimi has announced the development of a transparent rewards engine on the Sui network. According to NS3.AI, this system will feature a quest-based XP system, a real-time advertising leaderboard, and an auto-compounding feature. The initiative aims to enhance user engagement and streamline advertising processes on the platform.

data.title: Middle East Conflict Resolution Could Impact U.S. Treasury Yields
data.description: Daiwa Securities strategist Shun Otani suggests that if a resolution is reached in the Middle East conflict, the yield on the U.S. 10-year Treasury could decrease from its current 4.30% to approximately 4.20%. According to Odaily, Otani highlights that once hostilities subside, market attention may shift towards ensuring safe navigation through the Strait of Hormuz and repairing relations between Iran and Gulf countries, which have deteriorated during the conflict. He views the meeting of foreign ministers from over 40 countries regarding the situation in the strait and reports of negotiations between Iran and Oman as positive signals towards de-escalation.

data.title: Cosmos Ecosystem's Leap Wallet to Cease Operations in May
data.description: Cosmos ecosystem wallet application, Leap Wallet, is set to cease operations on May 28. According to BlockBeats, users are advised to complete their migrations promptly. The shutdown will also affect Compass Wallet (extension, iOS, Android), Leap WebApp, Swapfast, Leap Cosmos Hub validator node, and Leap Cosmos Snaps.

data.title: Xiaomi to Adjust Product Prices Amid Rising Component Costs
data.description: On April 3, Xiaomi announced that due to the significant increase in global storage chip and other key component prices, the company will adjust the suggested retail prices of some products starting April 11, 2026, at 00:00. According to Jin10, Xiaomi's President Lu Weibing stated on social media that the current surge in memory prices has exceeded expectations, with prices for the same version of memory rising nearly fourfold compared to Q1 last year. The price for 12+512 memory has increased by approximately 1,500 yuan, while the 16+1T version has seen an even more dramatic rise. This situation has greatly impacted REDMI, known for its competitive pricing strategy. As a result, Xiaomi will slightly increase or restore the retail prices of certain models.

data.title: Bittensor's Market Cap Surges as Subnet 3 Releases Covenant-72B
data.description: TAO's value nearly doubled in March, reaching approximately $317, which boosted Bittensor's market capitalization to just over $3 billion. According to NS3.AI, this increase coincided with the release of Subnet 3's Covenant-72B. The 72-billion-parameter model was trained across more than 70 distributed nodes and achieved a score of 67.1 on the MMLU benchmark, placing it competitively alongside Meta's Llama 2 70B. Currently, over 68% of the 10.7 million issued TAO is staked.

data.title: Hyperliquid's Market Share in Perpetual Futures Trading Approaches 6%
data.description: Hyperliquid has achieved a nearly 6% share in the perpetual futures trading market, with its monthly trading volume reaching $200 billion. According to NS3.AI, this increase in market share indicates that Hyperliquid's growth surpasses the general rise in trading activity.

data.title: Pixie Chess Secures $5.2 Million in Seed Funding
data.description: Pixie Chess, a Web3-based chess platform, has successfully raised $5.2 million in a seed funding round. According to Foresight News, the investment was led by Paradigm, with participation from SEED CLUB and several angel investors. The funding aims to enhance the platform's development and expand its reach in the digital chess community.

data.title: Trader loracle.hl is selling 450,000 HYPE tokens, worth $15.98 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, trader loracle.hl (@loraclexyz) is selling 450,000 HYPE tokens, worth $15.98 million. In the past 9 hours, he sold 311,900 HYPE tokens through limit orders, worth $10.97 million, leaving 138,100 HYPE tokens remaining, worth $4.91 million.

data.title: Gyeonggi Province Initiates Stablecoin Research Project
data.description: Gyeonggi Province has commissioned a research project to develop an implementation plan for a stablecoin. According to NS3.AI, this initiative represents the first instance of a local government formally ordering such a study following prior discussions at the council level. Officials stated that the project aims to prepare for the expansion of blockchain-based financial infrastructure and to engage in ongoing regulatory discussions.

data.title: Alibaba's Qwen3.6-Plus Achieves High Ranking on CodeArena's Global List
data.description: Alibaba's Qwen3.6-Plus has secured the second position on CodeArena's global programming model list. According to NS3.AI, this achievement places it ahead of models from OpenAI, Google, and xAI, making it the highest-ranked Chinese large model on the leaderboard.

data.title: Drift Protocol Vulnerability Expands to Affect 20 Protocols
data.description:
Recent data from SolanaFloor reveals that the impact of the Drift protocol vulnerability has widened, now affecting 20 protocols, up from the previous 11. According to Odaily, newly affected protocols include PiggyBank, Perena, Vectis, Valeo, Amp Pay, Loopscale, Prime Numbers Fi, Gauntlet, and Exponent.

In terms of financial losses, Prime Numbers Fi estimates losses exceeding $10 million, Gauntlet around $6.4 million, Neutral Trade approximately $3.67 million, Elemental DeFi about $2.9 million, Reflect Money around $1.95 million, Vectis approximately $1.69 million, Ranger Finance about $919,000, Pyra around $551,000, and PiggyBank confirmed a loss of $106,000, which the team has fully compensated.

Affected protocols have implemented various measures, with most suspending minting, redemption, deposits, withdrawals, or related vault functions. Prime Numbers Fi is still assessing the situation and has not announced specific actions. Vectis has yet to respond, but Ranger Finance has confirmed exposure to risk. Gauntlet has restricted further supply and is coordinating with Drift.

data.title: Hyperliquid Whale Positions Reach $3.473 Billion, Data Shows
data.description:
Hyperliquid platform whales currently hold positions totaling $3.473 billion, according to ChainCatcher. Data from Coinglass reveals that long positions account for $1.762 billion, representing 50.73% of the total, while short positions amount to $1.711 billion, making up 49.27%. Long positions have incurred a loss of $128 million, whereas short positions have gained $137 million.

A notable whale address, 0xa5b0..41, has taken a 15x leveraged long position on ETH at a price of $2,148.7, resulting in an unrealized loss of $6.9864 million.

data.title: Intergaze will be officially shut down. Users must complete asset withdrawal within 14 days prior to the bridge's closure.
data.description: According to the official announcement, Intergaze, the zero-gas-fee blockchain optimized for NFTs, will be officially shut down. All users must complete asset withdrawal within 14 days before the bridge closes. NFT holders' NFTs will be migrated to Stargaze on Cosmos Hub in the second half of May. Users need to register a Cosmos wallet before May 1st to ensure they are included in the migration list. The specific registration link will be announced later.

data.title: ECB and BOE Rate Predictions for 2026: Divergent Views Among Major Banks
data.description:
Major financial institutions have released their predictions for the European Central Bank (ECB) and the Bank of England (BOE) interest rate movements in 2026. According to Jin10, Barclays, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan, Morgan Stanley, Deutsche Bank, and UBS Global Research anticipate the ECB will raise its deposit facility rate, currently at 2%, to 2.5% through various increments in April, June, July, and September. In contrast, HSBC and Bank of America expect the ECB to maintain the rate at 2% throughout the year.

For the BOE, which currently has a rate of 3.75%, Barclays and Standard Chartered foresee a rate cut in the second quarter of 2026, aligning with previous expectations. JPMorgan predicts a rate increase to 4% in June, while UBS Global Research expects a rate cut to 3.5% in November. Citibank anticipates no change in rates, contrary to earlier forecasts of cuts in June and September. Bank of America projects rate hikes in June and July, reaching 4.25% by year-end, deviating from earlier expectations of rate cuts. Morgan Stanley and Goldman Sachs expect the BOE to maintain its rate, with Goldman Sachs predicting a gradual reduction to 3% next year.

data.title: The crypto market sector continued its correction, with the NFT sector falling nearly 4% and BTC dropping below $67,000.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the crypto market generally saw a pullback, with the NFT sector falling 3.85% in the last 24 hours. Within the sector, Pudgy APENFT (NFT) fell 0.58%, Penguins (PENGU) fell 2.13%, and ApeCoin (APE) fell 5.24%. Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC) fell 1.06%, dropping below $67,000. Ethereum (ETH) fell 2.52%, fluctuating narrowly around $2,000. In other sectors, the Meme sector fell 0.04% in the last 24 hours, with MemeCore (M) showing relative strength, rising 4.28%. The PayFi sector fell 1.16%, and Dash (DASH) fell 4.01%. The Layer 1 sector fell 1.45%, with Algorand (ALGO) bucking the trend and rising 7.61%. The Layer 2 sector fell 1.88%, but Polygon (POL) rose 0.66%. The DeFi sector fell 2.11%, with EdgeX (EDGE) surging 13.38% intraday. The CeFi sector fell 1.65%, with Binance Coin (BNB) falling 3.34%. The crypto sector index, reflecting historical sector performance, shows ssiDePIN rising 1.41%, while ssiCeFi and ssiLayer1 fell 3.01% and 2.05%, respectively.

data.title: Polymarket Partners with La Liga for Exclusive Prediction Markets
data.description: Polymarket has announced an exclusive partnership with La Liga to establish official match prediction markets for teams such as Real Madrid and Barcelona. According to NS3.AI, this collaboration marks the first time a major European football league has signed with a prediction market platform. The partnership aims to enhance fan engagement by offering unique insights into match outcomes and team performance.

data.title: Solana Spot ETFs See Net Inflows as Fidelity Leads
data.description: U.S. Solana (SOL) spot ETFs experienced a total net inflow of $932,900 on April 2. According to NS3.AI, the Fidelity Solana Fund ETF (FSOL) was the only fund to report a net inflow during this period.

data.title: Alibaba's 1000 Questions 3.6 Tops China's Strongest Programming Model
data.description: CodeArena, a subsidiary of LMArena focusing on AI programming capabilities and a globally renowned blind benchmarking platform for large AI programming models, released its latest rankings. Alibaba's latest generation large language model, Qwen3.6-Plus, ranked second globally, surpassing international giants such as OpenAI, Google, and xAI, becoming the highest-ranked Chinese large AI programming model on the list. (Jinshi)

data.title: Intergaze to Cease Operations, NFT Migration Planned to Stargaze
data.description: Intergaze, a zero gas fee chain optimized for NFTs, is set to gradually cease operations. According to Foresight News, users are required to withdraw their assets within 14 days before the cross-chain closure, specifically by April 16. NFT holders will have their assets migrated to Stargaze on the Cosmos Hub in the latter half of May. Users must register a Cosmos wallet by May 1 to be included in the migration process.

data.title: Japan's 2-Year Government Bond Yield Reaches Highest Level Since 1995
data.description: Japan's 2-year government bond yield has increased to 1.385%, marking its highest point since May 1995. According to NS3.AI, this rise reflects ongoing shifts in the financial markets, as investors adjust to changing economic conditions. The increase in bond yields is indicative of broader trends in the global economy, where interest rates and inflationary pressures are influencing investment strategies.
